children.name: Megalaima
children.description: Asian barbets — stout, colourful, fruit-eating birds with heavy, bristle-fringed bills. They are the Old World ecological counterparts of toucans, feeding primarily on figs and other fruit. Their calls are loud, repetitive whistles or trills that carry long distances through tropical forests. Found across South and Southeast Asia.

children.name: Psilopogon virens
children.description: The largest Asian barbet, found from the Himalayas to Indochina. Green body with a brown head, blue throat, and yellow eye patch. Its far-carrying 'tuk-oorr-tuk' call is a characteristic sound of Himalayan forests.

children.name: Psilopogon haemacephalus
children.description: The smallest Asian barbet, found from Pakistan through India to Southeast Asia. Green body with a red and yellow head. Named for its 'tuk-tuk-tuk' call that sounds like a coppersmith hammering metal. Common in gardens and urban parks.

children.name: Psilopogon asiaticus
children.description: A green barbet with a blue throat and red forehead patch. Found from the Himalayas to Southeast Asia. Its persistent 'tuk-tuk-tuk' call is often mistaken for a woodpecker. Feeds largely on figs and other fruit in the forest canopy.

children.name: Psilopogon lineatus
children.description: A large green barbet with a streaked white head and yellow throat. Found from the Himalayas through Southeast Asia to Java and Bali. Its call is a loud, repetitive 'puk-puk-puk'. Inhabits lowland and foothill forests.

children.name: Psilopogon frontatus
children.description: Found in the forests of the northern Western Ghats in India. Green body with a bright red forehead patch, yellow throat, and blue face. Restricted to mid-elevation evergreen forests.

children.name: Psilopogon auricularis
children.description: A barbet with golden whisker marks, found in Southeast Asia.

children.name: Psilopogon cyanotis
children.description: A barbet with blue ear patch, found in Southeast Asia.

children.name: Psilopogon duvaucelii
children.description: A barbet with black ear patch, found in the Malay Peninsula and Sumatra.

children.name: Psilopogon eximius
children.description: A barbet endemic to Borneo.

children.name: Psilopogon faber
children.description: A barbet found in southern China and Hainan.

children.name: Psilopogon flavifrons
children.description: A barbet with yellow forehead, endemic to Sri Lanka.

children.name: Psilopogon franklinii
children.description: A barbet with golden throat, found from the Himalayas to Vietnam.

children.name: Psilopogon henricii
children.description: A barbet with yellow crown, found in the Malay Peninsula and Sumatra.

children.name: Psilopogon incognitus
children.description: A barbet with moustache pattern, found in Southeast Asia.

children.name: Psilopogon javensis
children.description: A barbet with black band, endemic to Java and Bali.

children.name: Psilopogon lagrandieri
children.description: A barbet with red vent, found in Southeast Asia.

children.name: Psilopogon malayensis
children.description: A barbet found in the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, and Borneo.

children.name: Psilopogon monticola
children.description: A barbet found in the montane forests of Borneo.

children.name: Psilopogon muelleri
children.description: A barbet found in Borneo.

children.name: Psilopogon mystacophanos
children.description: A barbet with red throat, found in Southeast Asia.

children.name: Psilopogon nuchalis
children.description: A barbet endemic to Taiwan.

children.name: Psilopogon oorti
children.description: A barbet found in the montane forests of Sumatra.

children.name: Psilopogon pulcherrimus
children.description: A barbet with golden nape, endemic to Borneo.

children.name: Psilopogon pyrolophus
children.description: A barbet with fiery tuft, found in the Malay Peninsula and Sumatra.

children.name: Psilopogon rafflesii
children.description: A multi-coloured barbet found in the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, and Borneo.

children.name: Psilopogon rubricapilla
children.description: A barbet with crimson forehead, found in Sri Lanka and the Western Ghats.

children.name: Psilopogon spectabilis
children.description: A barbet endemic to Luzon in the Philippines.

children.name: Psilopogon steerii
children.description: A barbet endemic to Mindanao in the Philippines.

children.name: Psilopogon tristis
children.description: A barbet with yellow ear patch, endemic to Sri Lanka.

children.name: Psilopogon viridis
children.description: A barbet with white cheek, found in the Western Ghats of India.

children.name: Psilopogon zeylanicus
children.description: A barbet with brown head, found in Sri Lanka and India.

children.name: Psilopogon marshallorum
children.description: A barbet found in Southeast Asia.
